title: Combating Cybercrime, Fraud, and Predatory Schemes Against American Citizens
summary: Executive Order 14390, signed by the President on March 6, 2026, aims to combat cybercrime, fraud, and predatory schemes targeting American citizens. The order establishes a policy to protect Americans and harden financial and digital systems against these threats, which are often perpetrated by Transnational Criminal Organizations (TCOs) and sometimes supported by foreign regimes. Key directives include a review of existing frameworks to combat TCOs, the development of an action plan to disrupt these organizations, and the creation of an operational cell within the National Coordination Center (NCC). The plan will leverage technical capabilities and threat intelligence, including from the private sector, to enhance attribution and disruption. The Attorney General will prioritize prosecutions of cyber-enabled fraud, and the Secretary of Homeland Security will partner with the NCC to provide support and resilience-building to State, local, Tribal, and territorial partners. The order also mandates the Attorney General to recommend a Victims Restoration Program to provide restitution to victims from forfeited funds. Internationally, the Secretary of State will engage with foreign governments to demand enforcement actions against TCOs and impose consequences on nations that tolerate such activities, in coordination with allies.
